description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring multiple rectangular solar panels mounted on a flat, platform-like base. The panels are arranged in two distinct clusters, each set at a noticeable tilt to maximize sunlight exposure. The base appears to be constructed from a sturdy material, possibly metal or reinforced concrete, with raised edges and several structural supports. The solar panels themselves have a grid-like pattern typical of photovoltaic cells, and the installation includes several black mounting brackets and possible electrical housings. The overall design suggests a fixed, ground-mounted system suitable for urban environments such as rooftops, parking structures, or dedicated solar farms. No visible cabling or inverter units are apparent, and the structure is robust enough to support additional weight or equipment on its surface.
description: A robust solar energy installation with multiple tilted solar panels on a flat, raised base, featuring structural supports and grid-patterned photovoltaic cells.
description_view_0: Front view showing the two main clusters of tilted solar panels and the raised edges of the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt angles of the solar panels and the elongated, flat base structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back sides of the solar panels and the structural supports along the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the staggered arrangement of the solar panels and the mounting brackets.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 30-35 degrees, facing a consistent direction, with a fixed orientation.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with visible aluminum or steel frame supports and a flat base platform.
attribute_array_configuration: Two clusters of solar panels, each with multiple panels arranged in a staggered, linear configuration.
